executiveSummary.text: Flutter Entertainment (FLUT) is now back in sync with the canonical DYOR HQ sheet after the metadata repair pass. The current stance is REDUCE with conviction 50/100, using $102.38 as the latest stored price reference where available.

businessModel.text: Flutter Entertainment operates in online betting and gaming, where market share, customer economics, regulation and product execution drive returns. The repaired note replaces a generic placeholder with a coherent company-level summary.

recentCatalysts.text: US state launches, sportsbook hold, iGaming mix and cost control remain the most important near-term variables.

thesisEvaluation.text: Base case: the core operating backdrop remains good enough to support the present reduce stance, but not strong enough to justify a looser risk view without better evidence. Bull case: execution and demand improve faster than expected. Bear case: operating or funding pressure worsens and compresses valuation further.

keyRisks.text: Regulatory changes can affect growth and profitability. Customer acquisition can stay expensive. Sports results can create quarterly volatility. Competitive intensity can compress margins. Execution across multiple jurisdictions adds complexity.

risks.risk: Regulatory changes can affect growth and profitability.
Sports results can create quarterly volatility.
Execution across multiple jurisdictions adds complexity.

whoShouldOwn.text: Own: Investors who accept regulatory and quarterly volatility for digital gaming exposure. Avoid: Investors who want predictable cash flows and low headline risk.

recommendation.text: REDUCE - Conviction: 50/100. This rebuilt page is intentionally concise, but it is now internally coherent and aligned to the corrected ticker and company metadata.

entryExit.text: Keep position sizing consistent with a reduce stance until fuller company-specific analysis is refreshed.
